questions.text: The energy of an electron which accelerates through a potential difference of 1000 V is.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect as per formula; E=e×V
This is incorrect as per formula; E=e×V
This is incorrect as per formula; E=e×V

The energy of an electron after accelerating through a potential difference of 1,000 volts (V) can be calculated using the formula: E=e×Vwhere ( E ) is the energy in joules (J), ( e ) is the charge of the electron ( 1.6×10-19 ) coulombs), and ( V ) is the potential difference in volts.So for a potential difference of 1,000 V, the energy ( E ) would be: E=1.6×10−19C×1000V E=1.6×10−16J

questions.explanation: The energy of an electron after accelerating through a potential difference of 1,000 volts (V) can be calculated using the formula: E=e×Vwhere ( E ) is the energy in joules (J), ( e ) is the charge of the electron ( 1.6×10-19 ) coulombs), and ( V ) is the potential difference in volts.So for a potential difference of 1,000 V, the energy ( E ) would be: E=1.6×10−19C×1000V E=1.6×10−16J
hints: accelerates through a potential difference of 1000 V

questions.text: ‘A wire of uniform cross-sectional area ‘A’ and length ‘L’ is cut into two equal parts. The resistance of each part becomes.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula: R = ρ L/A

When a wire with uniform cross-sectional area ( A ) and length ( L ) is cut into two equal parts, the resistance of each part becomes ( R’ ). The resistance ( R ) of a conductor is given by the formula: R = ρ L/Awhere ( ρ ) is the resistivity of the material. When the wire is cut into two equal parts, the length ( L ) is halved (L/2), so the new resistance ( R’ ) of each part is: R’ = ρ (L/2)/A = 1/2 ρ L/A = 1/2 R or R/2Therefore, the resistance of each part becomes half of the original resistance.
Incorrect as per formula: R = ρ L/A
Incorrect as per formula: R = ρ L/A

questions.explanation: When a wire with uniform cross-sectional area ( A ) and length ( L ) is cut into two equal parts, the resistance of each part becomes ( R’ ). The resistance ( R ) of a conductor is given by the formula: R = ρ L/Awhere ( ρ ) is the resistivity of the material. When the wire is cut into two equal parts, the length ( L ) is halved (L/2), so the new resistance ( R’ ) of each part is: R’ = ρ (L/2)/A = 1/2 ρ L/A = 1/2 R or R/2Therefore, the resistance of each part becomes half of the original resistance.

questions.text: The maximum output power is delivered to a load resistance ‘R’, when the internal resistance ‘r’ of the source is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It is incorrect because of Maximum Power Transfer Theorem,
It is incorrect because of Maximum Power Transfer Theorem,
The maximum output power is delivered to a load resistance ( R ) when the internal resistance ( r ) of the source is equal to the load resistance ( R ), so ( r = R ).
It is incorrect because of Maximum Power Transfer Theorem,

questions.explanation: The maximum output power is delivered to a load resistance ( R ) when the internal resistance ( r ) of the source is equal to the load resistance ( R ). This is known as the Maximum Power Transfer Theorem, which states that to obtain maximum external power from a power source with internal resistance, the resistance of the load must equal the resistance of the source as viewed from its output terminals. In mathematical terms, the condition for maximum power transfer is ( r = R ).
hints: ‘r’ of the source is equal to

questions.text: Work done on a charged particle moving in a uniform magnetic field is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It is incorrect because the magnetic force is always perpendicular to the direction of motion of the particle.
It is incorrect because the magnetic force is always perpendicular to the direction of motion of the particle.

The work done on a charged particle moving in a uniform magnetic field is zero. This is because the magnetic force is always perpendicular to the direction of motion of the particle, and since work is the dot product of force and displacement, no work is done by the magnetic field on the particle
It is incorrect because the magnetic force is always perpendicular to the direction of motion of the particle.

questions.text: When carrying electricity on long distances, step-up transformers are used to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Step-up transformers are used in long-distance electricity transmission to increase the voltage and decrease the current. This process reduces energy losses during transmission, making it more efficient to transport electricity over large distances. Essentially, by stepping up the voltage, the current is reduced, which minimizes the heat generated due to resistance in the wires.
It is not possible because this process can't reduces energy losses during transmission, to make it more efficient to transport electricity over large distances.
It is not possible because this process can't reduces energy losses during transmission, to make it more efficient to transport electricity over large distances.
It is not possible because this process can't reduces energy losses during transmission, to make it more efficient to transport electricity over large distances.

This process reduces energy losses during transmission, making it more efficient to transport electricity over large distances. Essentially, by stepping up the voltage, the current is reduced, which minimizes the heat generated due to resistance in the wires.

questions.text: The capacitive reactance in a pure capacitive D.C circuit is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula; XC​= 1/ωC
Incorrect as per formula; XC​= 1/ωC
Incorrect as per formula; XC​= 1/ωC

The capacitive reactance in a pure capacitive DC circuit is infinite. This is because a steady DC voltage causes the capacitor to charge up and then block any further current, acting like an open circuit. Therefore, no current flows through the circuit once the capacitor is fully charged. XC​= 1/ωC As,​ ω = 2πf So, XC​= 1/2πf.C In the case of DC, where the frequency ( ω = 2πf ) is zero, the capacitive reactance (XC) becomes infinite, as dividing by zero yields infinity.

questions.explanation: The capacitive reactance in a pure capacitive DC circuit is infinite. This is because a steady DC voltage causes the capacitor to charge up and then block any further current, acting like an open circuit. Therefore, no current flows through the circuit once the capacitor is fully charged. XC​= 1/ωC As,​ ω = 2πf So, XC​= 1/2πf.C In the case of DC, where the frequency ( ω = 2πf ) is zero, the capacitive reactance (XC) becomes infinite, as dividing by zero yields infinity.

questions.text: if both the length and radius of the rod are doubled, then modulus of elasticity will:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It is not possible because it is an intrinsic property of a material, meaning it does not change with the dimensions of the material.
It is not possible because it is an intrinsic property of a material, meaning it does not change with the dimensions of the material.

When the length ( L ) and radius ( r ) of a rod are doubled, the new length and radius are ( 2L ) and ( 2r ) respectively. The modulus of elasticity ( E ) is defined as the ratio of stress to strain, and is given by: E= Stress/StrainBy solving it, we find that modulus of elasticity become half.But it is an intrinsic property of a material, meaning it does not change with the dimensions of the material. Therefore, if both the length and radius of the rod are doubled, the modulus of elasticity will remain unchanged.
It is not possible because it is an intrinsic property of a material, meaning it does not change with the dimensions of the material.

questions.explanation: When the length ( L ) and radius ( r ) of a rod are doubled, the new length and radius are ( 2L ) and ( 2r ) respectively. The modulus of elasticity ( E ) is defined as the ratio of stress to strain, and is given by: E= Stress/StrainBy solving it, we find that modulus of elasticity become half.But it is an intrinsic property of a material, meaning it does not change with the dimensions of the material. Therefore, if both the length and radius of the rod are doubled, the modulus of elasticity will remain unchanged.

questions.text: Which particle has the shortest wavelength, if all are Having the same velocity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula; λ = h/p​ = h/mv
Incorrect as per formula; λ = h/p​ = h/mv
Incorrect as per formula; λ = h/p​ = h/mv

The particle with the shortest wavelength, when all particles have the same velocity, is the one with the highest mass. This is because the de Broglie wavelength (\lambda) of a particle is inversely proportional to its momentum (p), which is the product of mass (m) and velocity (v). The relationship is given by the equation:λ= h/p​ = h/mvwhere (h) is Planck’s constant. So, for a constant velocity, the greater the mass of the particle, the smaller the wavelength.The mass of the particles in increasing order is: electron < proton ≈ neutron < alpha particle. Therefore, the wavelengths in decreasing order are: electron > proton ≈ neutron > alpha particle. So, the alpha particle has the shortest wavelength among the given particles when they all move with the same velocity.

questions.explanation: The particle with the shortest wavelength, when all particles have the same velocity, is the one with the highest mass. This is because the de Broglie wavelength (\lambda) of a particle is inversely proportional to its momentum (p), which is the product of mass (m) and velocity (v). The relationship is given by the equation:λ= h/p​ = h/mvwhere (h) is Planck’s constant. So, for a constant velocity, the greater the mass of the particle, the smaller the wavelength.The mass of the particles in increasing order is: electron < proton ≈ neutron < alpha particle. Therefore, the wavelengths in decreasing order are: electron > proton ≈ neutron > alpha particle. So, the alpha particle has the shortest wavelength among the given particles when they all move with the same velocity.
hints: if all are Having the same velocity

questions.text: If an object moves with speed of light its mass will be.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula; m= mo / √ 1−v2 /​c2
Incorrect as per formula; m= mo / √ 1−v2 /​c2

The formula that relates an object’s mass ( m ) to its velocity ( v ) as it approaches the speed of light ( c ) is given by the relativistic mass formula:m= mo / √ 1−v2 /​c2​​Here, ( m0 ) is the rest mass of the object, which is the mass measured when the object is at rest relative to the observer.As the velocity ( v ) of the object approaches the speed of light ( c ), the denominator of the Lorentz factor approaches zero, causing the relativistic mass ( m ) to approach infinity.
Incorrect as per formula; m= mo / √ 1−v2 /​c2

questions.explanation: The formula that relates an object’s mass ( m ) to its velocity ( v ) as it approaches the speed of light ( c ) is given by the relativistic mass formula:m= mo / √ 1−v2 /​c2​​Here, ( m0 ) is the rest mass of the object, which is the mass measured when the object is at rest relative to the observer.As the velocity ( v ) of the object approaches the speed of light ( c ), the denominator of the Lorentz factor approaches zero, causing the relativistic mass ( m ) to approach infinity.
hints: object moves with speed of light its mass

questions.text: The energy of the electron in the excited state n=3 in hydrogen atom is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula; En​=−13.6/n2​ eV

The energy of an electron in the excited state ( n=3 ) for a hydrogen atom can be calculated using the formula: En​=−13.6/n2​ eVPlugging in ( n=3 ), we get: E3​=−13.6​/(3)2 eV E3​=−13.6/9​ eV E3​=−1.51 eVSo, the energy of the electron in the excited state ( n=3 ) in a hydrogen atom is ( -1.51 eV ).
Incorrect as per formula; En​=−13.6/n2​ eV
Incorrect as per formula; En​=−13.6/n2​ eV

questions.explanation: The energy of an electron in the excited state ( n=3 ) for a hydrogen atom can be calculated using the formula: En​=−13.6/n2​ eVPlugging in ( n=3 ), we get: E3​=−13.6​/(3)2 eV E3​=−13.6/9​ eV E3​=−1.51 eVSo, the energy of the electron in the excited state ( n=3 ) in a hydrogen atom is ( -1.51 eV ).
hints: in the excited state n=3 in hydrogen atom

questions.text: The process of generating three-dimensional image of the object using laser beam is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Holography is a technique that uses laser beams to create a hologram, which is a three-dimensional image of an object.

A medical imaging technique that creates a 3D image of the inside of an object from a large series of two-dimensional X-ray images taken around a single axis of rotation. CT scans are a common example of tomographic imaging.
A process of analyzing a real-world object or environment to collect data on its shape and possibly its appearance (e.g., color). The collected data can then be used to construct digital 3D models.
This is a movie that enhances the illusion of depth perception, hence adding a third dimension. Special glasses are usually worn by viewers to see 3D movies in theaters.

questions.explanation: Holography is a technique that uses laser beams to create a hologram, which is a three-dimensional image of an object. The other three also create 3D image but they do not involve laser beam.
hints: generating three-dimensional image of the object using laser beam

questions.text: Mass equivalent of 931 MeV energy is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The mass equivalent of 931 MeV of energy can be calculated using Einstein’s mass-energy equivalence formula E = mc2 where ( E ) is energy, ( m ) is mass, and ( c ) is the speed of light in a vacuum.Given that; 1 electronvolt (eV) is equal to ( 1.6×10-19 ) joules (J), and 1 megaelectronvolt (MeV) is ( 106 ) eV we can convert 931 MeV to joules and then find the mass equivalent:931MeV = 931×106×1.6×10-19 =1489.6×10-13JNow, using the mass-energy equivalence formula: E = mc2 m = E/c2 = 1489.6×10-12J/(3×108m/s)2 = 1.662×10-27kgTherefore, the mass equivalent of 931 MeV of energy is 1.662×10-27kg.
Incorrect as per formula; E = mc2
Incorrect as per formula; E = mc2
Incorrect as per formula; E = mc2

questions.explanation: The mass equivalent of 931 MeV of energy can be calculated using Einstein’s mass-energy equivalence formula E = mc2 where ( E ) is energy, ( m ) is mass, and ( c ) is the speed of light in a vacuum.Given that; 1 electronvolt (eV) is equal to ( 1.6×10-19 ) joules (J), and 1 megaelectronvolt (MeV) is ( 106 ) eV we can convert 931 MeV to joules and then find the mass equivalent:931MeV = 931×106×1.6×10-19 =1489.6×10-13JNow, using the mass-energy equivalence formula: E = mc2 m = E/c2 = 1489.6×10-12J/(3×108m/s)2 = 1.662×10-27kgTherefore, the mass equivalent of 931 MeV of energy is 1.662×10-27kg.
